sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports experiencing persistent, sharp chest pain radiating to his left arm, which began approximately 2 hours ago. He states the pain is rated 7/10 in severity. He denies fever, shortness of breath, or nausea. He reports taking aspirin daily.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure is 140/90 mmHg. Heart rate is 88 bpm and regular. Oxygen saturation is 98% on room air. Auscultation reveals no murmurs. The skin is warm and dry.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient is presenting with acute chest pain, concerning for possible myocardial infarction.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Activate EMS. Obtain 12-lead ECG. Administer aspirin 325 mg. Start oxygen at 2L via nasal cannula. Monitor vital signs closely.
